_meta.keyKnowledge: We learn to construct questions that help us find information, explore possibilities and understand processes.

_source.w: We learn to construct questions that help us find information, explore possibilities and understand processes.
y: Well-constructed questions are the engine of all inquiry and investigation.
Different types of questions reveal different kinds of information and understanding.
Strong questioning skills make us better researchers, learners and thinkers.
eg: We construct research questions that are specific enough to be answerable.
We build probing questions that dig deeper into an idea — 'What else might be going on here?'.
We create process questions — 'How does this work?' �� to understand systems.
We practise refining vague questions into focused, useful ones.
We use questioning to explore possibilities — 'What if we changed this variable?'.
We evaluate our questions and improve them before beginning our investigation.
